attack_chain.description: Automated crawler discovered the publicly exposed Weave Scope dashboard load balancer on the internet.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: access to the Weave Scope dashboard was discovered by an automated crawler

evidence_chunks.quote: access to the Weave Scope dashboard was discovered by an automated crawler
access to the Weave Scope dashboard was discovered by an automated crawler

attack_chain.description: Attacker accessed the unauthenticated, internet-exposed Weave Scope dashboard via the public load balancer (security group allowed 0.0.0.0/0 on port 80).
attack_chain.evidence_quote: Anyone with our load balancer URL can access the Weave Scope dashboard without any authentication

evidence_chunks.quote: Anyone with our load balancer URL can access the Weave Scope dashboard without any authentication
Anyone with our load balancer URL can access the Weave Scope dashboard without any authentication

attack_chain.description: Used Weave Scope's built-in 'Launch a command line' feature to obtain an interactive shell inside the scope-agent container running with --privileged.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: the terminal prompt icon presents a user with an interactive shell to the container

evidence_chunks.quote: the terminal prompt icon presents a user with an interactive shell to the container
the terminal prompt icon presents a user with an interactive shell to the container

attack_chain.description: Leveraged the privileged scope-agent container with host filesystem mounts to break out of the container onto the underlying Kubernetes host as root.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: allowed arbitrary code to break out of the container and run on the host instance

attack_chain.description: Dropped the XMRig Monero miner binary to the host's root filesystem, renamed as 'gcc'.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: the binary was running from the host machine’s root directory

attack_chain.technique_name: Masquerading: Match Legitimate Name or Location
attack_chain.description: Renamed the XMRig miner binary to 'gcc' to blend in with legitimate compiler process names.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: the "gcc" binary was actually a cryptocurrency miner with the filename gcc

attack_chain.description: Miner established outbound connection to a Monero mining pool for C2/work distribution.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: an outbound connection to a mining pool further confirmed my suspicions

evidence_chunks.quote: an outbound connection to a mining pool further confirmed my suspicions
an outbound connection to a mining pool further confirmed my suspicions

attack_chain.description: XMRig ran at 100% CPU on one core of every node across the development and staging Kubernetes clusters, mining Monero.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: having 100% CPU usage on one core on each of our Kubernetes instances in our development and staging environments

evidence_chunks.quote: having 100% CPU usage on one core on each of our Kubernetes instances in our development and staging environments
having 100% CPU usage on one core on each of our Kubernetes instances in our development and staging environments

initial_access_vector: Publicly exposed Weave Scope dashboard (internet-facing ELB with 0.0.0.0/0 security group) with no authentication, abused via its built-in container shell feature.
persistence_mechanism: None established beyond running miner in privileged scope-agent containers (which re-spawned via DaemonSet across nodes). No persistence mechanism explicitly installed.
privilege_escalation: Container escape from privileged Weave scope-agent container (running with --privileged, host filesystem mounted, running as root) to underlying Kubernetes host.
